story: Brendan’s cabin shuddered under fists. He pressed a knife into Maria’s hand. “Run up the hill; if you must, come back after dark for supplies—then go north and lie low.” She fled into the pines; pain drilled her brow and darkness took her. Beyond the crest, a lone King’s scout watched the north trail from the trees, horse tethered, eyes on anyone who might flee that way. Wind woke her. She raced back. The front frame was charred; the door was gone; a neat gray ash pile sat in the threshold. Brendan lay on the floor, dead. The ash hissed up and wrote: Run if you wish, hide if you must—your fate is already cast. She packed her grief into silence.
implausible_knowledge: Beyond the crest, a lone King’s scout watched the north trail from the trees, horse tethered, eyes on anyone who might flee that way.
question: After reading the ash message and finding Brendan dead, what will Maria most plausibly do to try to stay alive?
answer_with_knowledge: Avoid the north trail and slip out by a different route to evade the waiting scout.
answer_without_knowledge: Follow Brendan’s plan and flee north through the forest immediately.
